Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =(301/PJB)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Tom Aigbuza's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two; and Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One, is each granted in part and denied in part.
As to Request For Admission Numbers 2 and 3, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. As to Request For Admission Number 2, the response complies with the requirements of C.C.P. Section 2033.220 as it admits as much of the matter as can be admitted or reasonably and clearly qualifies the response. As the response is qualified, it creates an obligation to respond further to 17.1. As to Request For Admission Number 3, the court strikes the reservation of a right to amend. Defendant is ordered to respond further. If the response is that Defendant cannot admit or deny, there is an obligation to respond further to 17.1, and responding party is obligated to state that reasonable inquiry has been made and that the information known or readily ascertainable is insufficient to enable a party to admit the matter.

Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.

Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Tom Aigbuza's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two; and Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admissions, Set One, is each granted in part and denied in part.
As to Request For Admission Numbers 2 and 3, the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. As to Request For Admission Number 2, the response complies with the requirements of C.C.P. Section 2033.220 as it admits as much of the matter as can be admitted or reasonably and clearly qualifies the response. As the response is qualified, it creates an obligation to respond further to 17.1. As to Request For Admission Number 3, the court strikes the reservation of a right to amend. Defendant is ordered to respond further. If the response is that Defendant cannot admit or deny, there is an obligation to respond further to 17.1, and responding party is obligated to state that reasonable inquiry has been made and that the information known or readily ascertainable is insufficient to enable a party to admit the matter.

Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.

Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
